summary: High‑energy '86 show with blazing Althea, soaring Stella Blue & tight rhythm
blurb: The band delivers a fiery first set highlighted by Hell In A Bucket and Althea, then rolls into a marathon second set with China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Saint Of Circumstance, and a soaring Stella Blue. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout while Brent’s keyboards sit perfectly in the mix.
review: December 30, 1986 at the Henry J. Kaiser Convention Center captured one of the Dead’s most electric late‑year performances. The first set bursts out with the thunderous “Hell In A Bucket” and a scorching “Althea,” and even a brief detour into “C.C. Rider” feels purposeful. By the second set the band is fully locked in: the classic “China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider” flows into a soulful “Saint Of Circumstance,” while Brent’s tasteful synth fills lift the “Estimated Prophet > Eyes Of The World” stretch into a soaring jam. Highlights keep climbing with an inspired “Stella Blue,” a gritty “Throwing Stones,” and a rousing “Not Fade Away.” Guest spot from the Neville Brothers adds extra color, and the encore’s “Johnny B. Goode” rounds out an evening that feels both tight‑musicianly and wildly adventurous. Across all available tapes the performance is consistently praised—Jerry’s guitar is described as “on fire,” Phil’s bass as “bombs,” and the dual drummers as crisp and supportive—making this a must‑listen for anyone chasing the high‑octane side of ’86.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six analyzed recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the same songs (Althea, Stella Blue, Hell In A Bucket, China > Rider, Eyes Of The World) and describing the band as “on fire,” “back with vengeance,” and “high‑energy.” Consistent acclaim across multiple soundboard sources yields a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture; crisp, superb balance and highlights the band’s high‑energy performance better than any other source.

key_highlights: Blazing “Althea” with Jerry’s scorching solo work.
Classic “China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider” segue that fuels the second‑set momentum.
Soaring “Stella Blue” and a powerful “Eyes Of The World” jam.
Guest appearance by the Neville Brothers adds a unique flavor.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia is repeatedly described as ‘on fire,’ delivering scorching solos on Althea and soaring leads on Music Never Stopped and Stella Blue; his tone stays thick and expressive throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides solid, front‑filling bass work (‘bass bombs’) and luscious lines that drive the Iko jam and later songs like Eyes Of The World.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s energetic vocals shine on Saint Of Circumstance and he adds a memorable rap ending; his feedback-laced phrasing on Stella Blue is noted as haunting, though lyric slips are mentioned on Johnny B. Goode.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland earns kudos for tasteful fills, clean synth‑organ work, and perfectly balanced keys that sit beautifully in the mix across the entire show.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming is crisp with nice fills, complementing the high‑energy second set and keeping the rhythmic foundation tight.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann delivers tight, driving beats especially on the high‑octane portions of the second set; his playing is highlighted as crisp and supportive.
